text.title: risk-assessment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for risk-assessment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too many risks in the register — 30+ active items.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Focus on the top 5-10. Everything below score 4 goes to an Accepted archive; it is still tracked but not in the active review table.

failure-mode.description: No contingency plans for high-priority risks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every risk with score 6+ needs a contingency plan with trigger, response, resources, and owner. A risk without a plan is just documentation of a known failure mode.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Founders rate their own product and team risks too low. An outside reviewer quarterly is the minimum check on this bias.

failure-mode.description: Probability theater: H/M/L scoring is anchored on gut feel; absolute probabilities are wrong by an order of magnitude.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use it for relative ranking only. Absolute probabilities require FAIR methodology (quantitative loss modeling).

failure-mode.description: Black-swan blindness: the framework over-weights known categories (market/product/team/financial/operational). Tail risks (founder health, geopolitics, model regulation) get dropped.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly add a "tail risks" section or use the pre-mortem pass to surface these omissions.

failure-mode.description: Mitigation inflation: every risk gets a generic "monitor + diversify" plan that nobody executes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an action verb + owner + measurable trigger. Ban "monitor", "diversify", and "stay agile" as sole mitigations.

failure-mode.description: Static drift: risk registers age fast — a 6-month-old register reflects a company that no longer exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Monthly reviews are mandatory. Keep monthly snapshots in git; the delta is the actual signal.

failure-mode.description: No ROI on mitigation: cost of mitigation rarely tracked vs. expected loss; teams over-spend on low-EV risks (compliance) and under-spend on high-EV ones (concentration in one channel).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If a mitigation costs more than prob x impact in dollars, accept the risk instead.

failure-mode.description: LLMs assign H/M/L confidently with no basis. Ungrounded probability scores inflate register quality perception.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the agent to cite a source or comparable case for any score. Reject rows where the probability is not grounded in evidence or a base rate.

failure-mode.description: Category bias: agents over-produce market and product risks and under-produce financial, legal, and vendor risks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce a quota: at least 2 risks per category. Reject submissions missing financial or operational rows.

failure-mode.description: Recency bias from web search: researcher agents will pull last week's headlines and over-weight them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-check against historical base rates. A single recent event does not change the underlying probability distribution.

failure-mode.description: Optimism leak from system prompts: if the brief is enthusiastic ("groundbreaking platform"), the agent inherits the tone and scores risks lower.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use a neutral, redacted brief for the risk pass, or flip the agent into adversarial role explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Mitigation handwaving: agents default to "improve monitoring" / "diversify" / "stay agile" — banned phrases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an action verb + owner + measurable trigger. Reject any mitigation that does not name a specific action and a specific person.

failure-mode.description: Risk register drift on re-runs: re-running the enumeration agent yields a different ID set every time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use stable hashes (slug of risk statement) instead of sequential IDs, or have the agent diff-merge instead of regenerate, to preserve register continuity across runs.

failure-mode.description: No human checkpoint on "Avoid" decisions. "Avoid" means killing a feature or market.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Never let an agent autonomously execute an Avoid response. Hard human-in-the-loop gate required. Log the decision and the reasoning.

failure-mode.description: Confidentiality leak: risk statements often contain customer names, vendor terms, or unlaunched plans.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run the scrubber agent before any external sharing or before piping into a 3rd-party SaaS.

failure-mode.description: Agent can't run the pre-mortem alone. Pre-mortems need divergent perspectives — solo agent runs collapse to one viewpoint.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use 3+ agents with different system prompts (skeptic, customer, competitor) and consolidate. A single-agent pre-mortem produces a list skewed by the single prompt's optimism level.
